mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that immediately pressures equity valuations and generates negative short-term market returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the moving average crossover fails to predict direction during periods of stable industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the consumer price index above 2.5% year-over-year forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising real personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable consumer borrowing that triggers immediate market corrections and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls while RSAFS monthly growth rises
failure_condition: The divergence pattern produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable consumer prices indicates healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, creating an optimal environment for equity appreciation over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases while CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0% YoY
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline during periods of stable inflation.
